---
title: Raphael Feser
subtitle: "PharmD | PhD Candidate<br>Computational Biophysics<br>University of Geneva"
description: "<p align='left'>I am currently a PhD candidate in <strong>pharmaceutical sciences</strong> at the University of Geneva. I study receptor function in drug-relevant systems through biomolecular modelling, and I also work on a melanoma clinical research manuscript arising from earlier applied biostatistics research.</p>"
image: moi2.jpg
format:
  html:
    toc: false
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 13em
  links:
    - text: ""
      icon: linkedin
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/raphael-feser/
    - text: ""
      icon: mortarboard-fill
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=-VsBayEAAAAJ&hl=en&oi=ao
    - text: ""
      icon: envelope
      href: mailto:raphael.feser@unige.ch
---

<meta name="description" content="Raphael Feser. PharmD and PhD candidate in pharmaceutical sciences and computational biophysics at the University of Geneva, studying receptor function through biomolecular modelling and melanoma clinical research arising from earlier applied biostatistics work.">

<meta name="google-site-verification" content="KCgw2pt--nBaqC9akJgiNdvZABMkconuRXWspqaCp4Y" />

<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "Person",
  "name": "Raphael Feser",
  "jobTitle": "PhD Candidate in Pharmaceutical Sciences",
  "affiliation": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "University of Geneva"
  },
  "url": "https://raphaelfeser.github.io/",
  "sameAs": [
    "https://www.linkedin.com/in/raphael-feser/",
    "https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=-VsBayEAAAAJ&hl=en&oi=ao"
  ]
}
</script>


### Research focus

Many medically important receptors are dynamic rather than rigid. Small molecular changes can alter their function, drug response, and downstream biological effects in ways that are difficult to observe directly.

My main focus is molecular modelling in drug-relevant receptor systems. I also work on a melanoma clinical research manuscript arising from earlier applied biostatistics research.

### Current PhD research

I study how small changes in signalling molecules can cause the same immune-cell receptor to trigger different cellular responses. Using CCR5 as a case study, I examine how these molecular differences reshape receptor behaviour and downstream effects, with implications for rational drug design.

```{=html}
<a class="btn-outline" href="science.html">Explore receptor project</a>
```

### Earlier research

This project began during an earlier internship in biostatistics and is now being prepared as a manuscript. We study whether sentinel lymph node biopsy is justified by measurable improvement in outcomes, given that surgical procedures also carry risks. We address this using time-to-event analyses in large melanoma cohorts.

```{=html}
<a class="btn-outline" href="survival.html">Explore melanoma project</a>
```

### How I study these questions

```{=html}
<div class="method-pills">
  <span class="method-pill">Molecular dynamics</span>
  <span class="method-pill">Enhanced sampling</span>
  <span class="method-pill">Free-energy calculations</span>
  <span class="method-pill">Machine learning</span>
  <span class="method-pill">Docking</span>
  <span class="method-pill">Biomolecular modelling</span>
  <span class="method-pill">Structural analysis</span>
  <span class="method-pill">Protein language models</span>
  <!-- <span class="method-pill">Cox models</span>
  <span class="method-pill">Propensity score methods</span> -->
  <span class="method-pill">Statistical simulation for method evaluation</span>
  <span class="method-pill">Survival analysis</span>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<details class="cv-details">
<summary class="cv-summary">Background and experience</summary>
<div class="cv-section">

  <div class="cv-group">
    <p class="cv-group-label">Education</p>
    <ul class="cv-list">
      <li><strong>PhD in Biomolecular Modelling</strong> &middot; University of Geneva <span class="cv-date">Ongoing</span></li>
      <li><strong>Federal Diploma in Pharmacy (PharmD)</strong> &middot; Swiss FDHA <span class="cv-date">2023</span></li>
      <li><strong>MSc in Pharmacy</strong> &middot; University of Geneva <span class="cv-date">2023</span></li>
    </ul>
  </div>

  <div class="cv-group">
    <p class="cv-group-label">Professional</p>
    <ul class="cv-list">
      <li>
        <strong>Pharmacist</strong> &middot; Amavita, Cornavin, Geneva <span class="cv-date">Jan-Oct 2025 &middot; Part-time</span>
      </li>
      <li><strong>Trainee Pharmacist</strong> &middot; Pharmacie de L'Ile</li>
      <li><strong>Teaching &amp; Research Assistant</strong> &middot; University of Geneva</li>
      <li><strong>Laboratory Assistant</strong> &middot; Firmenich SA</li>
    </ul>
  </div>

  <div class="cv-group">
    <p class="cv-group-label">Research</p>
    <ul class="cv-list">
      <li><strong>Master's Thesis in Computational Biophysics</strong> &middot; UCL &middot; Prof. Haider &amp; Prof. Gervasio</li>
      <li><strong>Research Assistant in Statistics</strong> &middot; UNIGE &middot; Prof. Guerrier</li>
      <li><strong>Research Internship in Biostatistics</strong> &middot; Melanoma Institute Australia &middot; Prof. Lo</li>
      <li><strong>Open Synthesis Network</strong> &middot; DNDi &amp; UNIGE</li>
    </ul>
  </div>

  <div class="cv-group">
    <p class="cv-group-label">Outreach</p>
    <p class="cv-outreach">Non-profit involvement in pharmacy student advocacy and global health. <a href="./about.html#outreach-activities" target="_blank">See more →</a></p>
  </div>

</div>
</details>
```
